source: Canonical corpus CAC-v1 (26 nodes, ACTIVE+HIGH only) applied to L1-L6 question

prior_verdict.basis: All 33 nodes including PARTIAL/MEDIUM/ABSENT; full A.1 crawl

reassessment_question: From canonical corpus only (ACTIVE+HIGH, 26 nodes), is the L1-L6 model CONFIRMED, MODIFIED, or REJECTED?
rationale: Canonical corpus (26 nodes, ACTIVE+HIGH) contains more evidence-dense artifacts than full A.1 set, but the key blocker (Stream 00.2 absence) is unchanged.
L3 and L4 fragments remain the best-supported L-number evidence in the canonical corpus.
The Three-Layer Analytical Model (CC-005) and 9-Stage Pipeline (CC-002) are both CONFIRMED from canonical corpus at HIGH confidence.
Without Stream 00.2 (MCA-001, CRITICAL), L0-L8 complete definition remains unverifiable.
Canonical corpus strengthens confidence in the MODIFIED verdict but cannot elevate it to CONFIRMED.

L1.notes: No canonical corpus node defines L1. Stream 00.2 would define it.

L2.notes: No canonical corpus node defines L2. Stream 00.2 would define it.

L3.best_anchor: CC-019 (44.1 Section 2: 'signals produced at L3, shaped at L4')
L3.notes: L3 = Signal Derivation. Fragment is explicit and appears in two canonical corpus nodes independently.

L4.best_anchor: CC-016 (43.1 Section 10: semantic shaping layer reference)
L4.secondary_anchor: CC-013 (41.1 semantic elevation confirms L4 functional zone)
L4.notes: L4 = Semantic Shaping. Explicit fragment in 43.1; functional zone confirmed by 41.x canonical corpus nodes.

L5.best_anchor: CC-016 (43.1 Section 10 negative assertion: 43.x/44.x does not hold L5/L6 authority implies L5 exists)
L5.notes: L5 = Presentation Assembly. Inferred from negative assertion. 43.x-44.x canonical corpus zone maps to L5 function but L5 label is not explicitly assigned.

L6.best_anchor: CC-016 (43.1 Section 10 negative assertion: similar to L5)
L6.secondary_anchor: CC-022, CC-023 (42.21, 42.22 runtime rendering zone confirmed)
L6.notes: L6 = Runtime Rendering. 42.x consumer execution zone maps to L6 function. Label inferred, not explicitly assigned.

L7_L8.notes: No canonical corpus node references L7 or L8.

nine_stage_pipeline.notes: Stages 1-7 fully confirmed in canonical corpus. Stages 8-9 (N-012, N-013) excluded as MEDIUM confidence (PARTIAL lifecycle).

three_layer_analytical_model.notes: All three layers (Observability, Intelligence, Executive Intelligence) fully represented in canonical corpus.

l_number_model_complete.notes: L3-L4 HIGH, L5-L6 MEDIUM from fragments only. L1-L2 absent. L7-L8 absent. Cannot confirm L1-L6 as a complete named model.

delta_from_a1.confidence_change: Marginal increase — canonical corpus filtering strengthens confidence in supported layers (L3, L4) by removing PARTIAL/MEDIUM noise. Does not change L1-L6 overall verdict.
delta_from_a1.new_findings: NONE — canonical corpus reassessment confirms A.1 conclusions without contradiction
delta_from_a1.resolved_ambiguities: NONE — AMB-001 (Stream 00.2 absent) remains open; MCA-001 is CRITICAL and unresolved

final_reassessment_verdict: MODIFIED — The canonical corpus reassessment produces no change from the A.1 MODIFIED verdict. The 9-Stage Pipeline and Three-Layer Analytical Model are CONFIRMED at HIGH confidence. L3-L4 are supported at HIGH confidence by canonical corpus evidence. L5-L6 remain MEDIUM (inferred). L1-L2 remain NOT DEFINED. The complete L1-L6 named model cannot be CONFIRMED without Stream 00.2.
